message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this plan through ONE lens. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

DIAGNOSIS: FOUR MEASURED CAUSES + TWO STRATEGIC ERRORS. Measured: (1) ATTRIBUTION INVISIBLE — all 240 [n] markers and the reference list are deleted by the RACE cleaner before judging (cleaner_survival_test.py: 0/12 survival), so the judge reads naked assertions on a task whose instruction is 'only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'; (2) CROSS-SOURCE INFERENCE BANNED BY CONSTRUCTION — entailment_judge.py NEUTRAL clause ('introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN', /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py) plus synthesis_contract.py:197-205 delete exactly the sentence class that wins INSIGHT (weight 0.32, our worst dim 0.4238); (3) PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE — 12 paragraphs, median 677 words, 0 H3 (verified vs rank10_sections_compose/report.md); readability 0.3774 is worst on the board and bleeds into the single-call judging of all other dims; (4) CORPUS MIS-AIMED — 997 rows/919 URLs but only ~17-22 on-topic journal articles, 5 rows with authors, grey-lit top domains; the PG_AGENTIC loop was never pointed at this task. Strategic errors in the brief that this plan corrects: (A) UNITS — 0.5578 is cellcog's 100-task corpus-wide score; cellcog has NO task-72 raw_results; regression estimate of its task-72 is ~0.556±0.019, and the highest MEASURED task-72 score is bodhi's 0.5441. (B) INVERSION — the brief frames cellcog's un-sourced synthesis as a weakness to out-verify; it is the engineered strength behind insight 0.5708 at weight 0.32 (a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' section of 8 named, epistemically tagged syntheses 'no single paper articulates'). The winning play is: copy cellcog's insight engine and monograph architecture, keep our deterministic no-fabrication floor underneath it, and out-gun it on evidence volume/recency/method-texture where the rubric verbatim pays for 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized'.
RETRIEVAL: JOURNAL-FIRST AGENTIC LOOP + CITATION-GRAPH SNOWBALL, STOPPED BY A COVERAGE MATRIX, NOT A COUNT. (a) QUERY-GEN: run the existing planner perspectives (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/agents/planner.py:309-531, PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES) but seed perspectives FROM the coverage matrix below, one per cell-group; queries phrased as scholarly-literature queries ('meta-analysis robot adoption employment', 'algorithmic management working conditions journal'), not news queries. (b) SEARCH: enable the idle loop — PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=1 (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/state.py:219), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS=15 (state.py:220, default 12), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_QUERIES=160 (state.py:221); INVERT the channel mix: PG_AGENTIC_ACADEMIC_PER_ROUND 2→8 and PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND 6→2 (state.py:229-230) so OpenAlex/Semantic-Scholar-class results dominate rounds (loop entry searcher.py:291). (c) EXPANSION — the decisive stage keyword search provably cannot do (Autor-Levy-Murnane's title contains neither 'AI' nor 'labor market'): after every 3 rounds, feed all newly found DOIs into /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py citation-graph expansion, BOTH directions (references + cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex), depth 2 from canonical anchors (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003; Acemoglu-Restrepo 2020; Frey-Osborne; Brynjolfsson-Mitchell-Rock; the 2024-25 meta-analyses). (d) FETCH: journal_corpus_fetch.py + deep_fetch.py (Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text); require retrievable abstract-or-fulltext, else keep metadata-only for context citations. (e) SELECT: hard gate = has DOI + journal + AUTHOR NAMES (the old corpus had 5/997 with authors — automatic reject now) + English; LLM on-topic gate scoring title+abstract against the task text (kills ResNet and Cognitive Psychology 1974); working papers (NBER/SSRN) quarantined into a 'contextual-only' bin, never primary evidence — this reproduces cellcog's Webb move. (f) WEIGHT: quality = journal standing + citation count + recency bonus (2024-2026, cellcog's refs are 41/104 from 2024+) + method-texture availability (does the abstract state N, design, identification?). (g) DEDUP: DOI-canonical; fuzzy-title for DOI-less. (h) CONSOLIDATE into evidence cards (next layer). STOPPING CONDITION = COVERAGE MATRIX, not a number: rows = restructuring dimensions the task names (employment/displacement, wages, skills/task content, polarization, autonomy/surveillance/algorithmic management, labor share, gender, Global South/informality, institutions/policy) x industries (manufacturing, healthcare, finance, retail, transport/logistics, professional services, education, platform/gig) x eras (computerization, robots/narrow AI, generative AI) x levels (platform/firm/regional/national/informal-sector). STOP when every reachable cell holds >=2 on-topic journal works OR a declared literature gap (gaps get narrated as findings — 'the peer-reviewed evidence on X is thin because...', which cellcog does and which no faithfulness gate can cut), AND total >=120 on-topic journal works with >=35% dated 2024+, AND one full round adds <2 new on-topic works per 20 queries. Target 120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100 — evidence volume is the lane where we out-score rather than match.
SYNTHESIS: THE CORRECTED-CORRECTED LINE. The brief's fix ('a mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source') is necessary but NOT sufficient — grounding proved it would delete cellcog's highest-value section (§5 opens: 'syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates'), and 71% of cellcog's sentences carry no citation at all. FOUR LEGAL SENTENCE CLASSES: (1) GROUNDED FACT — asserts what a cited card's span states; entailment as before. (2) COMPOSITE BRIDGE (bodhi's winning move) — finding from paper A + mechanism from paper B, BOTH cited; fix /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared. (3) ADJUDICATION — cited finding A + cited finding B + an uncited, HEDGED resolution that names the resolving dimension drawn from declared card fields (level/horizon/method): 'The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed workers face losses.' Legal iff the named dimension differs between the premise cards — deterministically checkable. (4) LABELED ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS (cellcog's insight engine, the class the brief's rule bans) — an un-sourced mechanism/claim is legal IFF all three hold: (i) HEDGED verb from an allowlist ('may', 'could', 'we propose as analytical synthesis', 'suggests'); (ii) EPISTEMICALLY TAGGED with the graded taxonomy ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Conceptual Argument] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]); (iii) accompanied by an EXPLICIT GAP DECLARATION verified against the gap index ('No peer-reviewed study has yet measured...' — and our corpus confirms none does). That is cellcog's fabrication-free mechanism verbatim: assert the original claim AND tell the judge it is original and untested. STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY, ALWAYS: any number not in a source span; any study/organisation/author that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any UNHEDGED-UNTAGGED mechanism no card states; forecasts stated as fact (hedged scenario-discussion tagged [Analytical Hypothesis] is legal; lunon's '2030 verdict' style is not); universals. The entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause stops being a sentence-killer and becomes a ROUTER: NEUTRAL sentences route to contract classes 2-4 instead of deletion.
COMPOSER: EXTEND /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py TO THE MEASURED #1 ARCHITECTURE — cellcog's actual moves, not the brief's misreadings. (1) CITATION TEMPLATE — cellcog's real workhorse (73 instances), NOT the brief's 'Writing in the JEP in 2019' contortion: 'Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, [verb] that ...' — author name and journal name in prose, YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted). Zero [n] in body; references section retained for provenance (cleaner deletes it; costs nothing). (2) SPINE = THREE TECHNOLOGY ERAS (computerization/polarization → robots/narrow AI → generative AI) with sectors NESTED inside each era — cellcog's actual skeleton. 4IR handled by critique-and-subordinate: adopt as periodization in §2, contest it with journal-published critiques, decline the frame uncritically; NO four-revolutions comparison spine (that is WhaleCloud's move, 0.5396). (3) STRUCTURE: ~10 H2 + ~30 H3 + 6-8 H4; median paragraph ~100w, max <280w; body 12,500-14,000 words (cellcog 13,486; any 8k cap fights #1 with half its ammunition). (4) STRUCTURED ABSTRACT Objective/Methods/Findings/Contributions with a THESIS stated up front — our analogue of cellcog's 'Heterogeneity, not displacement, is the central peer-reviewed finding', derived from our contradiction index; Contributions line enumerates the syntheses by count. (5) SCOPE/METHODS/SOURCE-SELECTION section that ARGUES compliance: narrates journal-only criteria, analyzes the constraint as a publication bias with a stated compensation strategy, pre-empts the working-paper objection — plus point-of-use re-enactment whenever a contextual-only card appears ('Because X remains a working paper, we reference it contextually rather than as primary evidence'). (6) THE INSIGHT ENGINE: a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' H2 with 6-8 NAMED, BRANDED syntheses ('the informality buffer', 'the gender exposure reversal' style), each opened with first-person ownership, epistemically tagged via the graded taxonomy, hedged, and gap-declared — a numbered inventory of insights handed to the INSIGHT judge (weight 0.32). Include the legend paragraph: 'The convention is not cosmetic...'. (7) ONE MASTER KEY threaded through everything — level-of-analysis/institutional mediation at five named levels — so the document has a thesis, not coverage. (8) MONOGRAPH WIRING: ~30 internal cross-references ('a tension taken up in Section 5.3') — zero board systems except cellcog do this; cheap to generate in a second pass. (9) EPIGRAMMATIC PARAGRAPH CLOSES: unit of composition = evidence → evidence → one-line citation-free abstraction. (10) OPENING/CLOSING RING: open by staking a concrete evidentiary tension as 'the organizing puzzle', close by resolving it verbatim; final sentence refuses to forecast and positions the review above both sides of the public debate. NO meta-commentary opening ('This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence...' is deleted from every template). (11) SECTOR TABLE: 8 sectors x dominant technology generation x primary finding x anchor studies — the instruction-coverage matrix rendered as content; thin cells narrated as gap-findings. (12) METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields.
EXPECTED: Honest sum from 0.4382, per-dimension, weighted (insight .32/compreh .29/instr .25/readability .14): READABILITY 0.3774 → ~0.520 (the board's observed ceiling — every non-POLARIS system sits 0.51-0.52; the fix is fully mechanical: 100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls) = +0.020 weighted. INSIGHT 0.4238 → ~0.555 (the four-class synthesis lane + the dedicated insight section + adjudication density + thesis; targets cellcog's own mechanism at its own dimension, cellcog=0.5708) = +0.042 weighted. COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 → ~0.555 (120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100, 13k body words, methods texture on every cited claim, sector matrix, recency through 2026 — the rubric's 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness' lanes, our genuine out-score lane) = +0.029 weighted. INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 → ~0.552 (journal-only compliance argued in prose and at point of use, working-paper quarantine, sectors covered visibly, 4IR engaged-and-critiqued; cellcog=0.5530) = +0.028 weighted. TOTAL ≈ 0.4382 + 0.119 ≈ 0.557. VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it — the central estimate beats bodhi's measured 0.5441 by ~4x the resolvable effect, but beating 0.5578 by a resolvable margin (mean >= 0.567) depends on the wheel converting the comprehensiveness-and-verification edge (more sources, newer sources, visible methods texture cellcog cannot exceed without our corpus) into +0.01 above the first-pass estimate. That is what iterations 3+ exist for, and the honest statement is: first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed.
CEILING: Three honesty items. (1) THE TARGET ITSELF IS SOFT: 0.5578 is cellcog's corpus-wide 100-task average; cellcog published no task-72 score. Regression across the 9 dual-scored systems puts its task-72 at ~0.556 with residual SD 0.0094 — the true target is ~0.556±0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441, possibly ~0.575. We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch). (2) READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52; the judge compresses this dim; budget no gains above it. (3) WHAT COULD KEEP US UNDER 0.5578: (a) the insight dimension may pay cellcog's 8 syntheses partly for genuine domain-expert quality, not just the labeled-hedged-gap FORM — if our class-4 sentences are formulaic, the judge's prose will say so and the ceiling is ~0.55; (b) retrieval may saturate below ~120 on-topic works if OA full-text coverage of 2024-2026 journals is poor — then comprehensiveness caps near parity with cellcog instead of above it; (c) cross-dimension bleed works both ways — a 13.5k-word report with any residual wall-of-text regressions taxes everything. If after the kill rule fires the plateau is 0.545-0.555, the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise — exactly the sentence the operator should get, not a proxy.

LENS:
RETRIEVAL REALITY: can this ACTUALLY reach ~100 on-topic canonical journal articles WITH usable text? Measured: keyword search returns junk; OpenAlex 429s our IP; ~half of paywalled papers have no OA copy (we recovered only 36/70). Is the corpus target achievable or a wish?

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""BUILD THE JOURNAL CORPUS by CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION — the "deep source" half of the SOTA plan.

WHY: the corpus audit found our 105 "sources" contain only 22 distinct real journal articles (~12
serious). cellcog, the #1 system on the board, has ~98. The task instruction is literally:
"Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."

WHY NOT KEYWORD SEARCH: tried and rejected, with evidence.
  - OpenAlex: hard 429 for this box's IP.
  - Crossref sorted by citations: returns ResNet and SMOTE — famous, not relevant.
  - Crossref sorted by relevance: returns papers with the keywords IN THE TITLE, which are the
    LOW-QUALITY ones (0-25 citations, near-predatory venues). The canonical papers do not have
    "artificial intelligence labor market employment" verbatim in their titles.

WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS: a real literature review is not assembled by search — it is assembled by
following the CITATION GRAPH out from the canonical papers. We already hold those anchors (Frey &
Osborne, Autor, Acemoglu & Restrepo, Eloundou, Noy & Zhang, Felten). Crossref returns each paper's
reference list, so we expand backwards from the anchors, then enrich and filter every candidate.

FABRICATION SAFETY: every field is copied verbatim from the Crossref record for that DOI. A work
whose author/venue/year cannot be resolved is DROPPED, never invented — the entire point of this
corpus is that we can NAME these papers in the prose, and the cleaner-survival test proved that only
journal-named prose survives RACE's cleaner.

Usage:
  python scripts/journal_corpus_build.py --target 100
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import re
import time
import urllib.parse
import urllib.request
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path

MAILTO = 'aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com'
UA = {'User-Agent': f'POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:{MAILTO})'}

# Our verified canonical anchors (from the Crossref-enriched bibliography).
# These are the papers any serious review of AI and the labour market MUST cite.
ANCHORS = [
    ('10.1016/j.techfore.2016.08.019', 'Frey & Osborne 2017, Technological Forecasting'),
    ('10.1257/jep.33.2.3',             'Acemoglu & Restrepo 2019, JEP'),
    ('10.1257/jep.29.3.3',             'Autor 2015, JEP'),
    ('10.1257/aer.103.5.1553',         'Autor et al. 2013, AER'),
    ('10.1126/science.adj0998',        'Eloundou et al. 2024, Science'),
    ('10.1126/science.adh2586',        'Noy & Zhang 2023, Science'),
    ('10.1002/smj.3286',               'Felten et al. 2021, Strategic Management Journal'),
    ('10.1016/j.hrmr.2022.100899',     'Chowdhury et al. 2023, HRMR'),
    ('10.1257/aer.96.2.189',           'Autor et al. 2006, AER'),
    ('10.1162/REST_a_00286',           'Baum-Snow & Pavan 2013, REStat'),
    ('10.1108/JOSM-04-2019-0088',      'Buhalis et al. 2019, J. Service Management'),
    ('10.1177/1042258720934581',       'Chalmers et al. 2021, ETP'),
]

# Venues that are NOT peer-reviewed journals, whatever Crossref's `type` says.
BAD_VENUE = ('ssrn', 'arxiv', 'preprint', 'working paper', 'conference', 'proceedings',
             'repository', 'mimeo', 'discussion paper')

# On-topic gate: the corpus must be about AI/automation/technology AND work/labour, not generic ML.
# NOTE: these are PREFIX patterns with NO trailing \b. A trailing boundary was a real bug here — it
# made "automat" fail to match "automation" and "computeris" fail to match "computerisation", which
# rejected Frey & Osborne, Acemoglu-Restrepo and Autor as "off-topic". Prefix-match, deliberately.
TOPIC_AI = re.compile(r'(artificial intelligence|\bAI\b|machine learning|machine|automat|robot|'
                      r'algorithm|generative|large language model|\bLLM\b|digital|computeris|'
                      r'computeriz|computer|technolog|industry 4\.0|fourth industrial revolution)', re.I)
TOPIC_WORK = re.compile(r'(labor|labour|employment|job|work|occupation|wage|skill|task|'
                        r'workforce|productiv|inequality|displac|polariz|polaris|firm|industr|'
                        r'human resource|organiz|organis)', re.I)


def get(url: str, timeout: int = 25, tries: int = 3):
    for i in range(tries):
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(urllib.request.Request(url, headers=UA), timeout=timeout) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read())
        except Exception:

except Exception:
            time.sleep(1.5 * (i + 1))
    return None


def crossref_work(doi: str) -> dict | None:
    d = get(f'https://api.crossref.org/works/{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}?mailto={MAILTO}')
    return d.get('message') if d else None


def refs_of(msg: dict) -> list[str]:
    return [r['DOI'].lower() for r in (msg.get('reference') or []) if r.get('DOI')]


def card(msg: dict) -> dict | None:
    """Verbatim card. Drop anything we cannot NAME — no guessing, ever."""
    au = [a.get('family', '').strip() for a in (msg.get('author') or []) if a.get('family')]
    venue = (msg.get('container-title') or [''])[0].strip()
    doi = (msg.get('DOI') or '').lower()
    title = (msg.get('title') or [''])[0].strip()
    typ = msg.get('type') or ''
    yr = None
    for k in ('published-print', 'published-online', 'issued'):
        parts = ((msg.get(k) or {}).get('date-parts') or [[None]])[0]
        if parts and parts[0]:
            yr = int(parts[0])
            break
    if not (au and venue and yr and doi and title):
        return None
    if typ != 'journal-article':
        return None
    if any(b in venue.lower() for b in BAD_VENUE):
        return None
    blob = f'{title} {venue}'
    if not (TOPIC_AI.search(blob) and TOPIC_WORK.search(blob)):
        return None
    who = au[0] if len(au) == 1 else (f'{au[0]} and {au[1]}' if len(au) == 2 else f'{au[0]} et al.')
    return {
        'doi': doi, 'title': title, 'authors': au[:6], 'venue': venue, 'year': yr,
        'citations': msg.get('is-referenced-by-count', 0),
        'type': typ,
        # THE format the cleaner-survival test proved survives RACE (journal named, year as PROSE):
        'attribution': f'Writing in the {venue} in {yr}, {who}',
        'attribution_short': f'{who} ({yr}), {venue}',
    }


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument('--out', default='outputs/journal_corpus.json')
    ap.add_argument('--target', type=int, default=100)
    ap.add_argument('--sleep', type=float, default=0.15)
    a = ap.parse_args()

    corpus: dict[str, dict] = {}
    stats = Counter()

    # 1. the anchors themselves
    print('=== STEP 1: the canonical anchors ===')
    anchor_msgs = {}
    for doi, label in ANCHORS:
        msg = crossref_work(doi)
        time.sleep(a.sleep)
        if not msg:
            print(f'  MISS  {label}')
            continue
        anchor_msgs[doi] = msg
        c = card(msg)
        if c:
            corpus[c['doi']] = c
            print(f'  ok    {c["citations"]:>6} cites  {label}')
        else:
            print(f'  (anchor kept as expansion seed only): {label}')

    # 2. expand backwards through their reference lists
    print(f'\n=== STEP 2: citation-graph expansion from {len(anchor_msgs)} anchors ===')
    candidates: Counter = Counter()
    for doi, msg in anchor_msgs.items():
        rs = refs_of(msg)
        for r in rs:
            candidates[r] += 1          # co-citation count = how many anchors cite it
        print(f'  {doi[:34]:<34} -> {len(rs):>3} referenced DOIs')

    ranked = [d for d, _ in candidates.most_common() if d not in corpus]
    print(f'\n  {len(ranked)} distinct candidate DOIs (ranked by how many anchors cite them)')

    # 3. enrich + filter each candidate
    print(f'\n=== STEP 3: enrich + filter (journal-article, on-topic, nameable) ===')
    for i, doi in enumerate(ranked, 1):
        if len(corpus) >= a.target:
            break
        msg = crossref_work(doi)
        time.sleep(a.sleep)
        if not msg:
            stats['crossref_miss'] += 1
            continue
        c = card(msg)
        if not c:
            stats['rejected'] += 1
            continue
        corpus[c['doi']] = c
        stats['accepted'] += 1
        if stats['accepted'] % 10 == 0:
            print(f'  ... {len(corpus)} in corpus (checked {i}/{len(ranked)})')

    out = sorted(corpus.values(), key=lambda c: -c['citations'])
    Path(a.out).write_text(json.dumps(out, indent=1))

    print('\n' + '=' * 76)
    print(f'=== JOURNAL CORPUS: {len(out)} distinct peer-reviewed journal articles ===')
    print(f'    we had 22.  cellcog (#1 on the board) has ~98.')
    print(f'    candidates checked: {stats["accepted"] + stats["rejected"] + stats["crossref_miss"]}'
          f' | accepted {stats["accepted"]} | rejected {stats["rejected"]} | crossref miss {stats["crossref_miss"]}')
    print(f'\n=== TOP 30 (the papers a real review MUST cite) ===')
    for c in out[:30]:
        print(f'  {c["citations"]:>6} | {c["attribution_short"][:64]}')
    print(f'\nwrote {a.out}')
    return 0


if __name__ == '__main__':

import urllib.request, json, time, re, random, collections
UA={'User-Agent':'POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com)'}
M='aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com'
def get(url,timeout=40,tries=2):
    for i in range(tries):
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(urllib.request.Request(url,headers=UA),timeout=timeout) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read())
        except Exception as e:
            last=e; time.sleep(1.0*(i+1))
    return None
TOPIC_AI = re.compile(r'(artificial intelligence|\bAI\b|machine learning|machine|automat|robot|algorithm|generative|large language model|\bLLM\b|digital|computeris|computeriz|computer|technolog|industry 4\.0|fourth industrial revolution)', re.I)
TOPIC_WORK = re.compile(r'(labor|labour|employment|job|work|occupation|wage|skill|task|workforce|productiv|inequality|displac|polariz|polaris|firm|industr|human resource|organiz|organis)', re.I)
BAD=('ssrn','arxiv','preprint','working paper','conference','proceedings','repository','mimeo','discussion paper')

cits=get('https://opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1/citations/10.1257/jep.33.2.3') or []
citing=sorted({c['citing'].lower() for c in cits})
print('COCI citing DOIs for A-R2019:', len(citing))
random.seed(7); sample=random.sample(citing, 90)
stats=collections.Counter(); accepted=[]
for doi in sample:
    m=get(f'https://api.crossref.org/works/{urllib.parse.quote(doi) if False else doi}?mailto={M}')
    time.sleep(0.05)
    if not m: stats['crossref_miss']+=1; continue
    msg=m['message']
    typ=msg.get('type'); venue=(msg.get('container-title') or [''])[0]
    au=[a.get('family') for a in (msg.get('author') or []) if a.get('family')]
    title=(msg.get('title') or [''])[0]
    yr=None
    for k in ('published-print','published-online','issued'):
        p=((msg.get(k) or {}).get('date-parts') or [[None]])[0]
        if p and p[0]: yr=int(p[0]); break
    if typ!='journal-article': stats['not_journal']+=1; continue
    if not (au and venue and yr and title): stats['unnameable']+=1; continue
    if any(b in venue.lower() for b in BAD): stats['bad_venue']+=1; continue
    blob=f'{title} {venue}'
    if not (TOPIC_AI.search(blob) and TOPIC_WORK.search(blob)): stats['offtopic_titlegate']+=1; continue
    stats['ACCEPT']+=1
    accepted.append({'doi':doi,'yr':yr,'venue':venue,'title':title,'abstract':bool(msg.get('abstract'))})
print('funnel over 90 sampled citing DOIs:', dict(stats))
yc=collections.Counter(a['yr'] for a in accepted)
print('accepted by year', sorted(yc.items()))
print('accepted 2024+:', sum(1 for a in accepted if a['yr']>=2024), '/', len(accepted))
print('crossref abstract present:', sum(1 for a in accepted if a['abstract']),'/',len(accepted))
for a in accepted[:15]: print('  ',a['yr'], a['venue'][:45],'|',a['title'][:70])
json.dump(accepted, open('/tmp/coci_accepted.json','w'))
EOF

input.command:
cd /tmp; timeout 400 python3 - <<'EOF'
import urllib.request, json, time, collections
UA={'User-Agent':'POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com)'}
def get(url,tries=3,timeout=20):
    for i in range(tries):
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(urllib.request.Request(url,headers=UA),timeout=timeout) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read())
        except Exception as e: time.sleep(1.5*(i+1))
    return None
acc=json.load(open('/tmp/coci_accepted.json'))
new=[a for a in acc if a['yr']>=2024]
st=collections.Counter()
for a in new:
    w=get(f"https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:{a['doi']}?mailto=aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com")
    time.sleep(0.35)
    if not w: st['oa_miss']+=1; print('  MISS', a['doi']); continue
    ai=w.get('abstract_inverted_index')
    words = sum(len(v) for v in ai.values()) if ai else 0
    st['has_abstract' if ai else 'no_abstract']+=1
    print(f"  {a['yr']} openalex_abs={'Y' if ai else 'N'} ({words}w) crossref_abs={'Y' if a['abstract'] else 'N'}  {a['venue'][:35]}")
print(dict(st), 'of', len(new))
# union with crossref abstract
EOF

content.text:
I've run live network probes and funnel measurements against the exact channels this plan depends on. Here's what the data says.

**Measured (today, from this box):**

| Channel | Status |
|---|---|
| OpenAlex **list/filter** (`?search=`, `?filter=cites:`) | **429 with 5x backoff, repeatedly** — dead |
| OpenAlex **single-entity** (`/works/doi:...`) | 200, 20/20 calls at 3/s — works |
| Crossref cited-by list | **does not exist** in the free API (only `is-referenced-by-count`) |
| Semantic Scholar (graph API, PDF + abstract fallbacks in `deep_fetch.py:78-88`) | **404 / 429** — dead |
| OpenCitations COCI | **works** (1334 citing DOIs for Acemoglu-Restrepo 2019; 296 for Eloundou; 2141 for Autor 2015) |
| Unpaywall | works |

**Live funnel, 90 randomly sampled COCI citing-DOIs of Acemoglu-Restrepo 2019, run through the plan's own gates (`journal_corpus_build.py` card()+topic gates):** 26 accepted (29%) → 19 of them 2024+. Of those 19: **5 OA (4 with PDF), 9 with any retrievable abstract (Crossref ∪ OpenAlex), 10 with NO retrievable text at all.** Every Elsevier venue in the recent on-topic set (Technological Forecasting, Technology in Society, Energy, China Economic Review, Finance Research Letters, Economic Analysis and Policy, J. Asian Economics) deposits **no abstract anywhere reachable**.

**Existing corpus state** (`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/journal_corpus_content.json`): 70 works, **content_status = 34 CITATION_ONLY / 24 ABSTRACT_ONLY / 12 FULLTEXT** → 36/70 groundable, and **year max = 2023, 2024+ count = 0** (backward-only reference expansion structurally cannot return papers newer than its anchors).

input.reasoning:
REFUTED on the retrieval-reality lens — the count is reachable, but "~100-150 canonical journal articles WITH usable text, >=35% dated 2024+, methods texture on every cited claim" is a wish, and the plan's decisive stage has no working implementation as written.

(1) BOTH FORWARD-CITATION CHANNELS THE PLAN NAMES ARE DEAD ON THIS BOX (verified live, today). Plan stage (c) — "the decisive stage keyword search provably cannot do" — is "cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex". Crossref's free API has NO cited-by list (only is-referenced-by-count). OpenAlex LIST endpoints (?search=, ?filter=cites:) return HTTP 429 on every attempt with 5x exponential backoff; only single-entity GETs (/works/doi:...) succeed (20/20 at 3/s). So the plan's stated expansion mechanism cannot run at all. It IS rescuable — OpenCitations COCI works and I confirmed it (1334 citing DOIs for Acemoglu-Restrepo 2019, 2141 for Autor 2015, 296 for Eloundou 2024) — but COCI is nowhere in the plan, and its coverage is Crossref-open-references-only (the Frey-Osborne query failed mid-stream with IncompleteRead). The plan as written is not executable; a repaired plan is.

(2) THE RECENCY LANE IS THE TEXT-POOR LANE. Live funnel: 90 random COCI citing-DOIs of A-R 2019 through journal_corpus_build.py's own gates -> 26 accepted (29%), 19 of them 2024+. Of those 19: 5 OA (4 with PDF), 9 with ANY retrievable abstract (Crossref ∪ OpenAlex single-entity), and 10 with NO retrievable text whatsoever. Every Elsevier venue in the on-topic recent set (Technological Forecasting, Technology in Society, Energy, China Economic Review, Finance Research Letters, Economic Analysis and Policy, J. Asian Economics) deposits no abstract to Crossref and has none in OpenAlex. Usable-text rate on the 2024+ lane ~= 47%, and the abstracts that do exist are ~98-250 words — they almost never state N, design, or identification strategy. Plan item COMPOSER-(12) "METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields" therefore cannot be satisfied from the card fields that will actually exist; it will either fire a deletion cascade or be silently relaxed.

(3) THE FETCH PIPELINE IS WEAKER TODAY THAN WHEN 36/70 WAS MEASURED. /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/journal_corpus_content.json: 70 works, 34 CITATION_ONLY / 24 ABSTRACT_ONLY / 12 FULLTEXT (36/70 groundable, exactly the "36/70" in the lens). Two of deep_fetch.py's three recovery paths are Semantic Scholar (semantic_scholar_pdf, deep_fetch.py:78; s2_abstract, deep_fetch.py:86) — S2 now returns 404/429 for this box. So a rebuild today degrades to Unpaywall+Crossref+OpenAlex-entity only. Scaling the measured rates: a 120-150-work corpus yields roughly 55-75 GROUNDABLE cards and ~15-25 with full text. That is not 2x cellcog's evidence; under our own no-fabrication contract, metadata-only cards can be NAMED but never used to state what a paper found — while cellcog (71% of sentences uncited) states findings for all ~100 of its refs from model knowledge. The claimed "genuine out-score lane" (COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 -> 0.555, +0.029 weighted) rests on groundable-source superiority that the measurement says will be parity at best.

(4) THE EXISTING CORPUS PROVES THE STRUCTURAL POINT: journal_corpus.json is 70 works with year max 2023 and ZERO from 2024+, because backward reference expansion cannot reach papers newer than its anchors. The ">=35% dated 2024+" requirement is 100% dependent on the forward channel in (1).

(5) RECENCY AND "HIGH-QUALITY" ARE IN DIRECT CONFLICT IN THE ONLY CHANNEL THAT WORKS. The reachable 2024+ set is not canonical: sampled accepted venues include Energy, Finance Research Letters, Emerging Markets Finance and Trade, Environment Development and Sustainability, Int. J. of System Assurance Engineering, J. of Chinese Economic and Business Studies, Scientific Reports — overwhelmingly China firm-panel robot econometrics with near-zero citations. Forcing 35%+ from 2024+ mechanically drags the corpus into a low-prestige tail on a task whose instruction is literally "only cites high-quality... journal articles," putting at risk the very INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING dimension the plan books +0.028 on. The quality weight in step (f) cannot manufacture canonical 2024+ works that do not exist in the reachable set.

(6) THE COVERAGE MATRIX CANNOT BE FILLED. The anchors are five economics-of-automation papers; forward citation from them cannot populate cells for algorithmic management/surveillance, gender, Global South informality, education, or healthcare. The only channel for those cells is keyword search — which journal_corpus_build.py's own header documents as returning ResNet, SMOTE, and near-predatory low-citation venues. The 9x8x3x5 (~1000-cell) matrix will terminate almost entirely through the "declared literature gap" escape hatch, so "stopped by a coverage matrix, not a count" degrades to "a count plus a large pile of gap declarations."

BOTTOM LINE: the 120-150 count is achievable (one anchor alone yields 1334 candidates at 29% gate-pass), but "canonical" and "with usable text" are not. Realistic ceiling on this box: ~120-150 named works of which ~55-75 are groundable, ~20 full-text, 2024+ share achievable but composed of mid-tier, uncited, abstract-less papers. Retrieval is not the out-score lane the EXPECTED section says it is; it is the plan's weakest link.
input.fix:
Repairs that make the retrieval layer honest and executable:
1. REPLACE the forward-citation channel: drop "cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex" (both dead) and wire OpenCitations COCI (https://opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1/citations/{doi}) into /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py as the forward edge, keeping Crossref /works/{doi} references as the backward edge. Handle IncompleteRead with streaming retries (Frey-Osborne fails on first read).
2. KEEP OpenAlex, but only as a SINGLE-ENTITY lookup (/works/doi:{doi}) for abstract_inverted_index — it works at 3/s and recovers ~4-5 abstracts per 19 recent works that Crossref lacks. Never call OpenAlex list/filter/search endpoints; they 429 unconditionally.
3. DELETE the Semantic Scholar fallbacks in scripts/deep_fetch.py:78-88 (404/429) or gate them behind an S2 API key; today they silently return nothing and inflate the perceived fetch budget.
4. REPLACE the ">=35% dated 2024+" hard rule with ">=35% dated 2024+ AMONG GROUNDABLE (abstract-or-fulltext) CARDS," and set the honest target at 120-150 NAMED works / 55-75 GROUNDABLE works, not 120-150 groundable. Report both numbers in the corpus audit so no downstream stage assumes text exists.
5. DOWNGRADE COMPOSER item (12): methods texture becomes "mandatory WHEN the card carries an abstract stating N/design/identification, else the claim is cited by title-and-venue only." Otherwise the synthesis contract will delete most 2024+ claims.
6. ADD a venue-standing floor to the recency bin (e.g. require the 2024+ works to clear a citation-percentile or venue-tier gate, or cap the low-tier China-panel share) so that satisfying recency does not violate the task's "high-quality journal articles" instruction.
7. RE-BASELINE the EXPECTED comprehensiveness gain: with groundable-source parity rather than 1.5x superiority, the +0.029 weighted on COMPREHENSIVENESS is not supported by the retrieval measurements; the honest first-pass band drops toward the low end (0.535-0.550) unless a working full-text channel (institutional access, publisher TDM, or paid S2/OpenAlex key) is added.
8. STOP the coverage matrix from lying: for cells only reachable by keyword search (gender, surveillance/algorithmic management, Global South informality, education, healthcare), declare them keyword-only up front and measure the junk rate before counting on them.
